raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I hired XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX, NC ) for a local move. I was quoted a 34 hour service at $ XXXX/hour. The move took nearly 8 hours, the crew was unprofessional, caused over {$1000.00} in damages to my belongings and walls, and failed to use basic protections ( no floor runners or padding ). I was charged {$2000.00}, and the final transaction amount posted to my American Express account on XX/XX/year>, in the amount of {$1800.00} ( after a {$100.00} deposit ). 

I submitted a dispute through Amex shortly after and provided documentation including : photos of damage, signed forms, and a detailed claim that XXXX XXXX XXXX refused to resolve fairly. Amex denied the dispute, claiming it was a tort claim not covered by the Fair Credit Billing Act. They also cited a generic delivery acknowledgment as proof that services were rendered. I called Amex customer service and was told I had no option to appeal the decision. I spoke with two representatives and both confirmed the case was closed with no recourse. 

This dispute was not about personal injury or unrelated damageit was a failure to provide services as quoted and advertised. I was billed for nearly double the estimated time, and the company refused to cooperate without demanding a non-disparagement agreement in return for a partial refund. 

I am requesting the CFPB review whether American Express followed fair dispute handling procedures, especially given that they refused to reopen or reconsider the case, and failed to acknowledge the dispute as a service delivery issue.
